title: Palo Alto Warns of Active Exploitation of PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN Flaw
blurb: Palo Alto Networks reported active exploitation of CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass flaw letting attackers reach PAN-OS GlobalProtect portals.

title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
blurb: Researchers at Varonis Threat Labs disclosed SearchLeak, a now-patched flaw that chained three bugs to let a single click on a trusted microsoft.com link exfiltrate emails, calendar data, indexed files, and MFA codes from Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search. Because the malicious link resolved to a legitimate Microsoft domain, conventional anti-phishing and URL-filtering tools failed to flag it.

title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
blurb: Attackers tampered with trusted JavaScript files distributed by the popular WordPress plugins PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse, turning them into a supply-chain backdoor. The modified code triggered only when a logged-in administrator loaded a page, silently creating an attacker-controlled admin account and installing a hidden plugin for persistent access while leaving ordinary visitors untouched.

title: Council of Europe hacked in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft heist
blurb: The extortion group ShinyHunters breached the Council of Europe by exploiting Oracle PeopleSoft vulnerabilities, adding the human-rights body to a victim list that includes the University of Nottingham and more than 100 other organizations. The campaign targets a widely deployed enterprise HR and finance platform, making the same flaws a live risk for any institution still running unpatched PeopleSoft installations.

title: Making Claude a Chemist
blurb: Anthropic shared research on improving Claude's performance in chemistry, training the model to reason through synthesis planning, molecular design, and other tasks that require specialized scientific knowledge. The work pairs capability gains with safety guardrails to limit dual-use risks, reflecting a broader push to make frontier models genuinely useful collaborators for working scientists.

title: Users cry foul after AMD stripped memory crypto from its consumer CPUs
blurb: AMD quietly removed Transparent Secure Memory Encryption (TSME) from its consumer Ryzen CPUs, leaving RAM contents unencrypted against cold-boot and physical attacks. Researchers found the change was undocumented and appears deliberate, pushing memory encryption into pricier Pro and server chips while consumer users lose a security feature they previously had.

title: Chinese hackers breach REDCap servers, steal medical research
blurb: China-linked hackers breached exposed REDCap servers to deploy InfiniteRed malware and steal medical research from a North American institution.

title: Salesforce reels in customer support AI specialist Fin for $3.6B
blurb: Salesforce acquired customer support AI startup Fin for $3.6B, adding autonomous support agents that the company claims resolve three-quarters of customer queries without human intervention. The deal deepens Salesforce's push to embed AI agents across its service products, intensifying competition with Zendesk and Intercom for the enterprise customer-support automation market.

title: PRC-linked spies hid inside medical and military networks for more than a year, snooping through Gmail and stealing data
blurb: Google says PRC-linked spies spent more than a year inside medical research and military networks, reading Gmail accounts and exfiltrating data on targets ranging from drone technology to pathogens. The prolonged dwell time underscores how state-backed intrusions increasingly prioritize patient, long-term access to sensitive research over smash-and-grab theft, leaving defenders to detect threats that hide in plain sight for months.

title: SpaceX’s biggest-ever IPO just grew to $85.7 billion raised
blurb: SpaceX's IPO grew to a record $85.7 billion raised after underwriters exercised their full allotment of shares. The maxed-out purchases push what was already the largest IPO in history even higher, underscoring investor demand for the rocket and satellite-internet company ahead of its public debut.

title: Fox to acquire Roku in $22B deal
blurb: Fox agreed to acquire Roku for $22B, a deal it says would create the third-largest television company in the United States. The acquisition pairs Fox's broadcast and sports assets with Roku's streaming platform and connected-TV operating system, giving the combined company a direct distribution channel to tens of millions of households.

title: FBI disrupts massive AI-powered phishing service using a million URLs
blurb: The FBI, working with Google and Black Lotus Labs, dismantled Outsider Enterprise, a China-based phishing-as-a-service operation that used AI to run more than a million fraudulent URLs and 9,000 fake websites since 2023. Authorities tied the campaigns to 3.8 million stolen credit card records and an estimated $1.9 billion in losses, seizing administration servers, a Shopify storefront, and roughly $100,000 in USDT as part of Operation Riptide.

title: Google says a Chinese-linked hacking group targeted US and Canadian academic, medical, and military research institutions from September 2023 to November 2025 (A.J. Vicens/Reuters)
blurb: Google's Threat Intelligence Group reported that a Chinese-linked hacking group targeted US and Canadian academic, medical, and military research institutions in a campaign running from September 2023 to November 2025. The two-year operation focused on stealing sensitive research data, reflecting a sustained state-aligned effort to compromise high-value institutions across both countries.

title: North Korean Hackers Are Turning Developer Tools Into Malware Delivery Channels
blurb: Proofpoint linked North Korea's Contagious Interview cluster to phishing campaigns that use developer-recruitment and code-review lures to deliver malware.

title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
blurb: Anthropic abruptly disabled its newly launched Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models Friday night after a US Commerce Department directive placed them under export controls barring use outside the United States. The administration cited concerns over a reported jailbreak of Fable 5's cybersecurity, chemistry, and biology safeguards, though Anthropic says the only evidence shown involves finding minor software flaws that models like GPT-5.5 can also surface.

title: Infinite Campus data breach affects 137,000 school staff accounts
blurb: ShinyHunters stole personal data from 137,000 Infinite Campus school staff accounts in a March Salesforce theft attack.

title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
blurb: SpaceX completed its IPO, becoming a publicly traded company and opening its financials to outside scrutiny for the first time. The newly public S-1 details pre-IPO deals, who stands to gain from the offering, and the business breakdown behind Starlink and launch operations that previously sat private.

title: Formal methods and the future of programming
blurb: Jane Street published an index of how it uses formal methods across its codebase, covering verification techniques applied to trading systems and core libraries. The series details where mathematical proofs and tools like model checking catch bugs that tests miss, and argues formal verification is becoming more practical for everyday software engineering.

title: How to earn a billion dollars
blurb: Paul Graham argues that the reliable path to earning a billion dollars is to start a fast-growing company and hold a large equity stake, since salaries and investing alone almost never reach that scale. He contends that the same forces driving startups also explain rising wealth concentration, and that the route is more learnable and less luck-dependent than most people assume.

title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
blurb: Anthropic said it will disable its most advanced models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the U.S. government ordered it to suspend foreign-national access under export controls, citing national security concerns tied to a reported Fable 5 jailbreak. The company called the order a "misunderstanding," noting the demonstrated technique surfaced only minor, already-known vulnerabilities that other public models can find without a bypass, and said it is working to restore access.

title: Arch Linux Now Believes Malware Incident Under Control: More Than 1,500 Packages
blurb: Arch Linux's AUR repository was hit this week by a malware campaign that compromised at least 1,579 user-contributed packages, escalating from an initial estimate of 400 over a single day. Developers say they have deleted all known malicious commits, but acknowledged the published list covers "many (but not all)" affected packages, underscoring the supply-chain risk of community-maintained software repositories.

title: The FCC Wants to Eliminate Burner Phones
blurb: The FCC proposed a rule that would effectively eliminate burner phones by requiring telecoms to store identifying data on nearly all customers, including a government ID number and physical address. Privacy advocates compare the plan to identity requirements in authoritarian states and warn of cybersecurity fallout, since mandating that carriers warehouse such data on every subscriber creates a sweeping new target for breaches.

title: 152 Chrome Wallpaper Extensions with 105K Installs Linked to Adware and Fake Traffic
blurb: Researchers found 152 Chrome live-wallpaper extensions with 105K installs that log user data and serve adware despite "no data collection" claims.

title: Canadian payments company Nuvei agrees to acquire NY-based cross-border payments company Payoneer for ~$2.75B in cash; the deal is expected to close in mid-2027 (Reuters)
blurb: Nuvei agreed to acquire cross-border payments company Payoneer for roughly $2.75B in cash, expanding the Canadian firm's reach in international money movement and small-business transactions. The deal is expected to close in mid-2027, pairing Nuvei's merchant payments stack with Payoneer's network serving freelancers and businesses across more than 190 countries.

title: Every Frame Perfect
blurb: Tonsky argues that UI quality should hold to a "every frame is perfect" standard, borrowed from Wayland: if you screenshot an app at any moment, including mid-animation, the result should be explainable. The post shows examples of janky intermediate frames and desynchronized animations in Safari and Photos, where components move out of step and erode user trust by signaling that the code may be equally unpolished.

title: New pancreatic cancer drug might open the door to much longer survival times
blurb: Researchers studying a new pancreatic cancer treatment found that targeting a single regulatory pathway shrank tumors and extended survival in early trials, suggesting they may have identified a "master switch" that drives the disease. Because the same mechanism appears across multiple cancer types, the finding could point toward therapies that work well beyond pancreatic tumors, which remain among the deadliest and hardest to treat.

title: EU sovereignty push gives tech buyers a new alphabet soup to swallow
blurb: The EU is advancing its digital sovereignty agenda, pushing cloud autonomy requirements and open-source mandates despite pushback from the US. The effort introduces a fresh layer of certifications, standards, and compliance frameworks that technology buyers must navigate when procuring services in Europe.

title: Sarvam becomes India’s newest AI unicorn with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech
blurb: Sarvam raised $234 million at a valuation above $1 billion, making the Bengaluru startup India's newest AI unicorn. IT services giant HCLTech led the round with a $150 million investment, deepening the link between India's legacy IT sector and its emerging foundation-model builders.

title: Copper transport drug restores memory and clears toxic Alzheimer's proteins
blurb: Monash University researchers reported that a copper-transport drug restored memory and cleared toxic amyloid and tau proteins in mouse models of Alzheimer's disease. The compound works by redistributing copper to brain regions where it is depleted, reactivating enzymes that break down the disease-linked proteins, though the results have yet to be replicated in human trials.

title: Source: Anthropic was given 90 minutes to comply and was not provided with detailed concerns before the export control order was issued (Financial Times)
blurb: The US gave Anthropic 90 minutes to comply with an export control order and did not share detailed concerns before issuing it, according to a source cited by the Financial Times. The compressed timeline and lack of advance explanation point to growing friction between frontier AI labs and regulators over how export rules are applied to model providers.

title: Anthropic’s Safety Superpower
blurb: Anthropic released Fable 5, a guardrailed version of its Mythos model that it had earlier withheld over advanced cybersecurity capabilities, only for the model to be jailbroken shortly after launch. The US government then issued an export control directive citing national security, forcing Anthropic to abruptly disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers while its other models remain available.

title: Pretrained to Imagine, Fine-Tuned to Act: The Rise of World-Action Models
blurb: Nvidia's developer blog details the emergence of World-Action Models, robot policies that pair a pretrained world model that imagines future states with a fine-tuned action head that executes tasks. The post traces the shift from vision-language-action models, which adapt a vision-language backbone into a control policy, toward architectures that first learn to predict environment dynamics and then ground that prediction in physical actions.

title: Electronic Arts launches EA Advertising, letting brands integrate directly into gameplay through dynamic, real-time placements, including stadium signage (Assiatou Hann/CNBC)
blurb: Electronic Arts launched EA Advertising, a platform that lets brands buy dynamic, real-time placements inside gameplay, including stadium signage and other in-world surfaces. The move opens a new revenue stream for EA as it integrates ads directly into live game environments rather than relying on traditional pre-roll or menu-based spots.

title: GLM 5.2 Is Out
blurb: Zhipu released GLM-5.2, its most capable open-source model to date, with a 1M-token context window and a focus on long-horizon agentic and coding tasks. The model is available now to all GLM Coding Plan tiers, with API access following next week, as Zhipu positions fully open weights against recent restrictions on closed frontier models.

title: Improving Arm64 support in CNCF projects with OCI credits
blurb: Oracle's $3M in OCI Arm64 credits gives CNCF projects self-hosted GitHub Actions runners to fill native CI gaps.

title: AWS rolls the dice for faster, more efficient networking
blurb: AWS detailed Resilient Network Graphs (RNG), a datacenter networking design that swaps traditional hierarchical topologies for a flat layout built on random graph theory, paired with a routing algorithm called Spraypoint and an optical Shufflebox that scrambles fiber connections. The company says RNG runs up to a third faster and 40% more energy efficient, and has deployed it in Ireland, Germany, and Spain with plans to reach most datacenters by year-end.

title: Google found liable for bad AI Overview results. Let’s play Truth Or Consequences
blurb: A court found Google liable for false statements generated by its AI Overviews, rejecting the company's argument that summaries auto-assembled from web sources are not its own speech. The ruling is the first to hold a search provider responsible for defamatory AI output, and it opens the door to a wave of similar claims as AI-generated answers increasingly replace traditional search links.

title: US clampdown on Anthropic models sends EU sovereignty surge into overdrive
blurb: New US export restrictions on Anthropic's models are accelerating European demand for sovereign AI alternatives, according to The Register. The clampdown is pushing EU enterprises and governments toward locally hosted models and domestic providers, reinforcing a broader push to reduce dependence on US-controlled AI infrastructure amid escalating transatlantic tech-policy tension.

title: All the news about Anthropic’s new AI fight with the White House
blurb: Anthropic is now fighting the White House on two fronts, after a June 12th order blocked foreign access to its newly released Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models. The directive lands on top of the company's existing standoff with the Pentagon, escalating tensions just days after the models launched June 9th with claims that Fable 5 outperforms any rival system.

title: Cybersecurity vets protest ‘dangerous’ US government ban on Anthropic’s most powerful models
blurb: Seventy-six cybersecurity experts, including Alex Stamos and Katie Moussouris, signed an open letter urging the US government to reverse its export control order on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models. The Friday order, citing unexplained national security concerns, prompted Anthropic to suspend the models worldwide, and the signatories argue it strips defenders of the best tools for finding vulnerabilities while adversaries advance.

title: Amazon CEO's talks with U.S. officials triggered crackdown on Anthropic models
blurb: A Wall Street Journal report says Amazon CEO Andy Jassy's conversations with U.S. officials helped prompt a government crackdown on the use of Anthropic's models. The episode is notable because Amazon is one of Anthropic's largest backers, having invested billions, underscoring how competing commercial and regulatory interests are shaping which AI systems reach federal customers.

title: Salesforce to Acquire Fin (formerly Intercom) for $3.6B
blurb: Salesforce signed a definitive agreement to acquire Fin, the customer-service AI platform formerly known as Intercom, for $3.6B. The deal folds Fin's AI support agents into Salesforce's Agentforce push, deepening its bet that autonomous agents will handle the bulk of frontline customer service.

title: Rio de Janeiro's "homegrown" LLM appears to be a merge of an existing model
blurb: Researchers allege that Nex-N2, a large language model promoted as a homegrown effort tied to Rio de Janeiro, is largely a merge of existing open-weight models rather than an original training run. The GitHub issue documents weight and architecture overlap as evidence, reviving long-standing concerns about model-provenance claims and how easily fine-tuned or merged releases can be marketed as ground-up development.

title: NHS patients can't opt out of Palantir's data platform – but their hospital can
blurb: NHS patients can't opt out of Palantir's Federated Data Platform, though individual hospital trusts can choose not to join.

title: Introducing the OpenAI Partner Network
blurb: OpenAI launched the Partner Network, committing $150M to help consultancies, integrators, and other partners move enterprise customers from AI pilots into production deployments. The program signals that the bottleneck for enterprise adoption has shifted from model capability to implementation, with OpenAI funding the services layer needed to drive transformation at scale.

title: SpaceX IPO raised a total of $85.7B as underwriters exercised their overallotment of shares, after raising an initial $75B on June 11 (CNBC)
blurb: SpaceX's IPO raised $85.7B after underwriters exercised their overallotment option, adding to the $75B raised in the initial June 11 offering. The total makes it one of the largest public listings on record, giving the company a fresh war chest to fund Starship development and Starlink expansion.

title: Salesforce acquires AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6B
blurb: Salesforce acquired AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6B, bringing the startup's team and technology in-house. The company plans to fold Fin into Agentforce, its enterprise platform for building custom AI agents that automate tasks, deepening Salesforce's bet on autonomous customer support.

title: A satellite just learned to find things on its own — here’s what that means
blurb: An Earth observation satellite autonomously identified its target on orbit for the first time in April, processing imagery onboard rather than downlinking raw data for ground analysis. The capability lets spacecraft prioritize and act on what they see in real time, cutting the bandwidth and latency that normally separate a satellite's observations from a usable result.

title: Show HN: Kage – Shadow any website to a single binary for offline viewing
blurb: Kage clones any website to a single offline binary, rendering pages in headless Chrome before stripping out all JavaScript.

title: Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
blurb: At Washington's request, Anthropic took its newest models offline over the weekend after the White House demanded it block access for all foreign nationals, including the company's own non-American employees. The abrupt shutdown underscored how thoroughly US policy controls frontier AI access abroad, strengthening the argument overseas for building sovereign, non-American models outside Washington's reach.

title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
blurb: Nvidia is seeking to raise over $25B in its first bond sale since 2021, marking a sharp turn for a company that has long funded itself from operating cash flow. The deal will test investor appetite for more AI exposure amid a wave of debt issuance across the sector, as chipmakers and hyperscalers borrow heavily to finance the buildout.

title: Running DOS on Behringers DDX3216 with a DIY x86-Bios from Scratch
blurb: A developer wrote a DIY x86 BIOS from scratch to boot FreeDOS on a Behringer DDX3216 mixer's AMD Elan SC300 386 processor.

title: Meta’s new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
blurb: Meta launched "AI Mode" on Facebook, a natural-language search that uses Meta AI to synthesize answers from public posts across Groups and Reels instead of returning a list of results. The feature follows Meta's Forum app and a wave of recent Facebook AI tools, part of a push to boost engagement and close its gap with rivals in the AI race.

title: Radical Numerics, which is developing AI models that learn directly from biological data, raised a $50M seed led by Emergence Capital (Natalie Breymeyer/Axios)
blurb: Radical Numerics raised a $50M seed round led by Emergence Capital to build AI models that learn directly from biological data. The startup is betting that training on biology, rather than text scraped from the web, can unlock applications in drug discovery and life sciences that general-purpose models miss.

title: I indexed 669 GB of my GoPro videos using my M1 Max computer and local ML models
blurb: A developer indexed 669 GB of personal GoPro footage entirely on an M1 Max using local ML models, making the entire archive searchable without sending anything to the cloud. The pipeline extracts frames, runs them through local vision models to generate captions and embeddings, then enables semantic search across the videos, demonstrating that consumer hardware can handle large-scale multimedia indexing offline.

title: Meta launches new AI features, including an "AI Mode" for search that uses Meta AI to surface answers pulled from public posts across the platform (Lauren Forristal/TechCrunch)
blurb: Meta launched a suite of new AI features, headlined by an "AI Mode" for search that uses Meta AI to generate answers drawn from public posts across its platforms. The move pushes Meta into conversational search territory occupied by Google and OpenAI, while turning its vast trove of user-generated content into a real-time answer engine.

title: As AI companies race to go public, who else is along for the ride?
blurb: SpaceX completed the largest IPO ever this week, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire and signaling a shift in where public-market capital flows. With Anthropic and OpenAI having confidentially filed to go public, analysts are recasting the old FAANG grouping as "MANGOS" (Meta, Anthropic, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, SpaceX), as money moves away from consumer and social platforms toward AI labs and deep tech.

title: RTX 5080 and RTX 3090 Setup: 80 Tok/s on Qwen 3.6 27B Q8
blurb: Pairing an RTX 5080 with a refurbished RTX 3090 to hit 80+ tok/s on Qwen 3.6 27B Q8, including BIOS and driver setup.

title: Cybersecurity startup Chainguard, Cisco, Cloudflare, JPMorgan Chase, and others launch Athena, a coalition to secure open-source software using AI (Rachel Metz/Bloomberg)
blurb: Chainguard, Cisco, Cloudflare, JPMorgan Chase, and other firms launched Athena, a coalition that uses AI to find and fix vulnerabilities in open-source software. The group aims to harden the widely used but underfunded open-source dependencies that underpin most commercial software, addressing supply-chain risks that have driven major breaches in recent years.

title: Google Chrome is closing the loopholes that let old ad blockers keep working
blurb: Google Chrome versions 150 and 151, expected in late June and July, will cut off the last workarounds that keep older Manifest V2 ad blockers running, according to 9to5Google. Google began phasing out Manifest V2 extensions like uBlock Origin in 2024, pushing users toward the more limited Manifest V3 versions or to rival browsers that still support the original tools.

title: Police officer investigated for using AI to 'create evidence' in multiple cases
blurb: A Derbyshire police officer is under investigation for allegedly using AI to fabricate evidence across multiple cases, according to Sky News. The case highlights how generative tools can corrupt the integrity of legal proceedings, raising fresh questions about evidence verification and oversight as AI becomes accessible inside institutions that demand a strict chain of custody.

title: UK unveils sweeping social media ban for users under 16
blurb: The UK proposed a ban barring users under 16 from major social media platforms, including TikTok, Snapchat, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, and X. The measure would force platforms to verify user ages and could reshape how they handle young users in one of Europe's largest markets, following a similar restriction enacted in Australia.

title: ReactOS (FOSS "Windows") achieves 3D-accelerated Half-Life on real hardware
blurb: ReactOS, the open-source operating system that has chased binary compatibility with Windows for 28 years, ran the Windows build of Half-Life with 3D acceleration on real hardware for the first time. A user booted the game on a Dell OptiPlex with a Core i5-2400 and an NVIDIA GeForce 8400GS, marking progress past earlier reports where the game only initialized.

title: Free SQL→ER diagram tool, runs in the browser, nothing uploaded
blurb: Free, open-source browser tool that turns SQL CREATE TABLE statements into interactive ER diagrams; nothing leaves your machine.

title: AI coding at home without going broke
blurb: A guide to AI coding at home: blend frontier subscriptions for planning with cheap API-rate open models for mechanical work.

title: Your ePub Is fine
blurb: A developer traces why Kobo e-readers reject technically valid ePub files, finding the failures originate in Adobe's RMSDK rendering engine rather than the books themselves. The post documents how Adobe's outdated EPUB parser, still embedded in many dedicated e-readers, enforces quirks that diverge from the spec, leaving authors to debug errors they didn't cause.

title: [AINews] Fable and Mythos officially too dangerous to release
blurb: Anthropic suspended Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 worldwide after a disputed US export-control order, reigniting debate over closed-API sovereignty risk.

title: Honda Civics and the Evil Valet
blurb: Honda Civic headunits accept USB updates signed with the public AOSP test key, enabling arbitrary code execution with physical access.

title: Good news—we have extra time before the Sun ends life on Earth
blurb: New modeling finds Earth's biosphere has roughly 1 billion to 1.6 billion years left before the brightening Sun renders the planet uninhabitable, extending earlier estimates. The work hinges on whether rising solar luminosity will first overheat plants or choke them by depleting atmospheric carbon dioxide, with the answer shifting the timeline for when complex life can persist.

title: Russia appears set to finally address long-term, serious space station cracks
blurb: Russia appears ready to repair the long-running air leaks in the aft section of the Zvezda module on the International Space Station, after years of disagreement with NASA over the severity of the cracks. The fix targets a slow but persistent loss of cabin air that has been a quiet point of tension between Roscosmos and NASA, raising questions about how much longer the aging station can safely operate.

title: Israeli firm BlackCore suspected of meddling in New York and Scotland votes
blurb: Reuters reports that Israeli firm BlackCore is suspected of covertly influencing votes in New York City and Scotland, expanding a list of alleged election-interference operations that already included French contests. The case highlights how private intelligence companies now run cross-border manipulation campaigns for commercial clients, raising fresh questions about the regulation and detection of for-hire political meddling.

title: Anthropic's belief in its own commitment to safety gives Anthropic the license to aggressively favor its business and even challenge the US government (Ben Thompson/Stratechery)
blurb: Ben Thompson argues that Anthropic's conviction in its own safety mission has become a justification for commercial aggression, letting the company favor its business interests and even push back against the US government. He contends this self-assurance creates a moral license problem, where believing you are the responsible actor rationalizes behavior that would otherwise invite scrutiny.

title: Fire burns Google Cloud India’s network, which remains slow a week later
blurb: A fire damaged Google Cloud India's network, which has remained slow a week later.

title: Yserver: A modern X11 server written in Rust
blurb: Yserver is a from-scratch X11 server in Rust that runs full MATE, XFCE, and Cinnamon desktops on modern Linux while dropping legacy Xorg baggage.

title: Britain plots digital bedtime after kicking under-16s off social media
blurb: Britain is drafting a nighttime social media curfew for minors, following its move to bar under-16s from the platforms.

title: Show HN: Trace – Offline Mac meeting transcripts you can flag mid-call
blurb: Trace transcribes Mac meeting audio entirely on-device, letting you flag key moments mid-call with a keystroke and export clean markdown.

title: A US judge dismisses xAI's lawsuit alleging OpenAI stole trade secrets, saying xAI failed to show OpenAI induced a former xAI engineer to divulge trade secrets (Jonathan Stempel/Reuters)
blurb: A US judge dismissed xAI's lawsuit accusing OpenAI of stealing trade secrets, ruling that xAI failed to show OpenAI induced a former engineer to divulge confidential information. The decision lets xAI refile an amended complaint, leaving the door open for further litigation in the escalating rivalry between Elon Musk's AI startup and OpenAI.

title: Salesforce reels in customer support AI specialist Fin for $3.6B
blurb: Salesforce acquired Fin, a customer support AI specialist, for $3.6B to expand its automated service offerings. Fin claims its AI agents can resolve roughly three-quarters of customer queries without human intervention, strengthening Salesforce's push to embed autonomous agents across its enterprise platform.

title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
blurb: Anthropic said it will disable its most advanced models, Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5, after the U.S. government ordered it to suspend access for all foreign nationals on national security grounds. The company said it believes the order stems from a misunderstanding over a Fable 5 jailbreak and is working to restore access, while other Claude models remain unaffected.

title: Source: Anthropic was given 90 minutes to comply and was not provided with detailed concerns before the export control order was issued (Financial Times)
blurb: Anthropic was reportedly given just 90 minutes to comply with a US export control order and was not provided with detailed concerns beforehand, according to a source cited by the Financial Times. The compressed timeline and lack of specifics point to an unusually abrupt enforcement process around AI-related export restrictions.

title: Sarvam becomes India’s newest AI unicorn with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech
blurb: Sarvam raised $234 million in a round led by HCLTech, reaching unicorn status as India's newest AI startup. The Bengaluru company received $150 million of that total from the IT services firm, which is backing Sarvam's push to build sovereign foundation models for the Indian market.

title: Noise infusion banned from statistical products published by Census Bureau
blurb: The US Department of Commerce ordered the Census Bureau and Bureau of Economic Analysis to stop using "noise infusion," the addition of calibrated random values that prevents published statistics from leaking individual records. The author argues the ban removes the core safeguard behind differential privacy, which the Census adopted for 2020 after finding the prior swapping method let attackers reconstruct confidential responses.

title: US clampdown on Anthropic models sends EU sovereignty surge into overdrive
blurb: The EU is intensifying its push for technological sovereignty after US export controls restricted European access to Anthropic's models. Brussels officials cited the curbs as evidence that the bloc must reduce dependence on American AI providers, accelerating investment in domestic compute, models, and infrastructure to insulate critical systems from foreign policy shifts.

title: Cybersecurity vets protest ‘dangerous’ US government ban on Anthropic’s most powerful models
blurb: Dozens of cybersecurity experts urged the White House to lift export-control restrictions on Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models. The group argues the order will hamper defenders' ability to secure their software and products, leaving them at a disadvantage against attackers who can still access comparable capabilities.

title: Introducing the OpenAI Partner Network
blurb: OpenAI launched the Partner Network, committing $150M to help consulting, technology, and service partners accelerate enterprise AI adoption and deployment. The program is designed to give partners resources and support for building, integrating, and scaling AI transformation projects across global customers.

title: Google found liable for bad AI Overview results. Let’s play Truth Or Consequences
blurb: A court found Google liable for defamatory AI Overview results, marking one of the first rulings to hold a company responsible for false statements generated by its AI search summaries. The decision raises the prospect of broader liability for AI-generated content, potentially exposing platforms to defamation claims over hallucinated outputs that previously fell under safe-harbor protections.

title: All the news about Anthropic’s new AI fight with the White House
blurb: Anthropic is now facing a second clash with the US government, after a June 12th order directed it to block foreign access to its newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5. The fight follows an existing standoff with the Pentagon and comes days after the models launched on June 9th, when Anthropic claimed Fable 5's capabilities exceed those of any rival system.

title: Salesforce to Acquire Fin (formerly Intercom) for $3.6B
blurb: Salesforce signed a definitive agreement to acquire Fin, the customer service AI platform formerly known as Intercom, for $3.6B. The deal folds Fin's agentic support automation into Salesforce's Agentforce and Service Cloud lineup, expanding its push to embed autonomous AI agents across enterprise customer service workflows.

title: Rio de Janeiro's "homegrown" LLM appears to be a merge of an existing model
blurb: Developers found that Nex-N2, marketed as a homegrown large language model from Rio de Janeiro, appears to be a fine-tune or merge of an existing open-weight model rather than an original build. A GitHub issue documenting the overlap drew 398 points and 218 comments on Hacker News, reviving scrutiny of "sovereign AI" projects that rebrand existing models as in-house work.

title: GLM 5.2 Is Out
blurb: Zhipu released GLM-5.2, its most capable open-source model to date, which supports a 1M-token context window and leads on long-horizon agentic task completion. The model is available tonight to all GLM Coding Plan tiers, including Lite, Pro, and Max, with API access going live next week.

title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Enables Full AI Gateway Takeover from Default Account
blurb: Three chained vulnerabilities in LiteLLM, a widely deployed open-source AI gateway, let a default low-privilege user bypass authorization, escalate to admin, and execute arbitrary code on the server. Obsidian Security researchers disclosed the CVSS 9.9 chain, which exposes every provider API key, decryption secrets, and all prompts flowing through the proxy, and maintainer BerriAI shipped complete fixes in version 1.83.14-stable.

title: Public and Private Medical Community Targeted by China-Nexus Threat Actor Pursuing Artificial Intelligence, Cyber, Medical, and National Defense Research
blurb: Google Threat Intelligence Group identified UNC6508, a China-nexus threat actor that spent over a year inside North American academic, medical, and military research institutions, exfiltrating data via compromised REDCap servers and custom INFINITERED malware. The group sought sensitive research spanning artificial intelligence, uncrewed vehicle systems, cyber offensive programs, and Indo-Pacific defense intelligence before Google disrupted its infrastructure and notified affected organizations.

title: Salesforce acquires AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6B
blurb: Salesforce acquired AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6B, bringing its team and technology into the company's Agentforce enterprise platform. Salesforce plans to use Fin's capabilities to strengthen Agentforce, which lets businesses build custom AI agents that automate customer service and other workflows.

title: Amazon CEO's talks with U.S. officials triggered crackdown on Anthropic models
blurb: A WSJ report says Amazon CEO Andy Jassy's conversations with U.S. officials helped trigger a government crackdown on the use of Anthropic models. The account is notable given Amazon's position as a major Anthropic investor and cloud partner, suggesting tension between commercial backing and the lobbying that shapes federal AI procurement decisions.

title: RTX 5080 and RTX 3090 Setup: 80 Tok/s on Qwen 3.6 27B Q8
blurb: This post documents a dual-GPU local LLM build pairing an RTX 5080 with a refurbished RTX 3090 to reach 80+ tok/s on Qwen 3.6 27B at Q8 quantization. It covers the required Asus X570 motherboard for 2x8 PCIe splitting, the BIOS settings for above-4G decoding and ReSize BAR, and why NVIDIA's patched P2P driver won't work across mismatched GPU generations.

title: Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
blurb: At Washington's request, Anthropic took its newest and most powerful AI models offline over the weekend after the White House demanded it block access for all foreign nationals, including its own non-American employees. The episode underscored US dominance over frontier AI and strengthened the case abroad for building sovereign, non-American models outside the reach of Washington's export and access controls.

title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
blurb: Nvidia is seeking to raise more than $25B through its first bond sale since 2021, marking a notable shift for a company that has largely avoided debt. The offering will test investor appetite for additional AI-sector exposure amid a wave of heavy borrowing across the industry.

title: Accelerating researchers and developers building multilingual AI with a new open dataset
blurb: GitHub published the GitHub Multilingual Repositories Dataset, a CC0-licensed metadata dataset that helps researchers find public repositories with non-English README, issue, and pull request content across more than 40 million repositories. Each entry classifies the language of three text sources using fastText, gcld3, and lingua-py with confidence scores, deliberately kept separate so users can tune precision for lower-resource languages.

title: AI OSS tool repo goes archived over night after raising $7.3M Seed
blurb: TensorZero, the open-source LLMOps platform whose gateway, observability, evaluation, and optimization stack fuels roughly 1% of global LLM API spend, archived its GitHub repository overnight shortly after raising a $7.3M seed round. The abrupt archival of a project used by companies from frontier AI startups to the Fortune 10 has raised questions about whether the company is shifting away from its open-source model.

title: As AI companies race to go public, who else is along for the ride?
blurb: SpaceX went public this week in the largest IPO ever, and competitors OpenAI and Anthropic have both confidentially filed to follow with their own public market debuts. The wave is reshaping the public markets, with one analyst recasting the old FAANG acronym as MANGOS, swapping out consumer names like Netflix for AI labs Anthropic and OpenAI.

title: A satellite just learned to find things on its own — here’s what that means
blurb: An Earth observation satellite identified targets from natural language queries on its own in April, marking the first reported use of a vision-language model in orbit. The demonstration ran Google DeepMind's Gemma 3 on NASA JPL's NAVI-Orbital software aboard Loft Orbital's YAM-9, which carries an Nvidia Jetson Orin AGX GPU to triage sensor data before downlinking it to analysts.

title: Don't trust large context windows
blurb: Models advertise million-token context windows, but this post argues you shouldn't trust them to actually use all of it. Retrieval accuracy degrades as context fills, with information buried in the middle frequently ignored or misremembered, so stuffing everything into the prompt produces worse, not better, results. The author recommends keeping context lean and curating what the model sees rather than relying on raw window size.

title: Meta launches new AI features, including an "AI Mode" for search that uses Meta AI to surface answers pulled from public posts across the platform (Lauren Forristal/TechCrunch)
blurb: Meta launched a suite of new AI features, including an "AI Mode" for search that uses Meta AI to surface answers pulled from public posts across its platforms. The mode draws on Facebook and Instagram content to generate responses directly within search, extending Meta AI's reach into a core discovery surface used by billions.

title: Radical Numerics, which is developing AI models that learn directly from biological data, raised a $50M seed led by Emergence Capital (Natalie Breymeyer/Axios)
blurb: Radical Numerics raised a $50M seed round led by Emergence Capital to build AI models that learn directly from biological data. The company is targeting foundation models trained on biology rather than text, an approach aimed at surfacing patterns standard language models miss.

title: Inside Ineffable Intelligence: the team behind David Silver's $5.1bn superintelligence push
blurb: Sifted profiled Ineffable Intelligence, the startup behind DeepMind researcher David Silver's $5.1bn superintelligence push. The piece details the team assembled around Silver, best known for his work on AlphaGo and reinforcement learning, and the bet that scaling those methods is the path toward building superintelligent systems.

title: I indexed 669 GB of my GoPro videos using my M1 Max computer and local ML models
blurb: A developer indexed 669 GB of personal GoPro footage entirely on an M1 Max laptop using local ML models, building a searchable archive without cloud services. The pipeline extracts frames, generates embeddings, and runs semantic queries on-device, demonstrating that consumer hardware can handle large-scale video understanding without sending data to external APIs.

title: Introducing Omnigent: A Meta-Harness to Combine, Control and Share Your Agents
blurb: Databricks open sourced Omnigent under Apache 2.0, a meta-harness that sits above existing coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Pi to make them interoperable parts of one system. It adds a common interface for composing and swapping agents, policy-based controls, and live collaboration with teammates, targeting the orchestration problems that begin where a single harness stops.

title: [AINews] Fable and Mythos officially too dangerous to release
blurb: Anthropic suspended access to its newly released Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all customers worldwide after a US government directive cited a potential jailbreak as a national cybersecurity risk. Anthropic disputes the underlying capability report, noting it received only verbal evidence of a narrow, non-universal jailbreak and that comparable capabilities exist in other models, including GPT-5.5.

title: Police officer investigated for using AI to 'create evidence' in multiple cases
blurb: A Derbyshire police officer is under investigation for allegedly using AI to fabricate evidence across multiple cases. The case raises questions about chain-of-custody and verification standards as generative tools become accessible inside law enforcement, where doctored documents or images could compromise prosecutions and erode trust in submitted evidence.

title: Anthropic's belief in its own commitment to safety gives Anthropic the license to aggressively favor its business and even challenge the US government (Ben Thompson/Stratechery)
blurb: Ben Thompson argues that Anthropic's conviction in its own safety mission has become a license to act in its commercial self-interest, justifying moves that favor its business and even put it at odds with the US government. The author contends this self-belief lets the company frame competitive and political maneuvering as principled, blurring the line between genuine safety commitments and strategic positioning.

title: Shepherd's Dog: A Game by Fable
blurb: A developer used Anthropic's new Fable model to one-shot a playable 2,319-line game in 45 minutes for over €20 in tokens.

title: Fine-Tuning Biological Foundation Models with LoRA Using NVIDIA BioNeMo Recipes
blurb: NVIDIA BioNeMo Recipes provide step-by-step training workflows for fine-tuning biological foundation models like ESM2 and Evo 2 using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), which trains roughly 1% of parameters while keeping the pretrained backbone frozen. The post walks through two case studies running on a single RTX 6000 Blackwell GPU: ESM2-3B for protein secondary structure prediction and Evo2-1B for DNA splice-site classification.

title: A US judge dismisses xAI's lawsuit alleging OpenAI stole trade secrets, saying xAI failed to show OpenAI induced a former xAI engineer to divulge trade secrets (Jonathan Stempel/Reuters)
blurb: A US judge dismissed xAI's lawsuit alleging OpenAI stole trade secrets, ruling that xAI failed to show OpenAI induced a former xAI engineer to divulge confidential information. The decision leaves room for xAI to amend and refile, but as it stands removes one of the legal pressure points between Elon Musk's startup and the company he has separately sued over its for-profit conversion.

title: Zhipu's shares closed up 33% on Monday after JPMorgan raised the stock's price target and picked it as a winner against MiniMax; MiniMax closed up 7% (Bloomberg)
blurb: Zhipu's shares closed up 33% on Monday after JPMorgan raised its price target and named the company a winner over rival MiniMax, whose shares closed up 7%. The note signals investor preference among China's leading large language model developers as both court enterprise customers and public-market capital.

title: Fox to acquire Roku in $22B deal
blurb: Fox agreed to acquire Roku for $22B, a move it says creates the third-largest TV company in the US. The deal hands Fox a streaming distribution layer and a connected-TV ad business it lacked, while Roku's platform suddenly sits inside a traditional broadcaster rather than competing as a neutral aggregator. For founders building on streaming or CTV ad infrastructure, the consolidation narrows the field of independent platforms and tilts leverage further toward the few companies that now own both content and the screen it plays on.

title: Meta reportedly moves to unwind $2B Manus deal after Beijing’s demand
blurb: Meta has begun unwinding its $2B acquisition of Manus after Beijing ordered the deal reversed, making the Chinese government a direct veto on cross-border AI M&A. The reversal strands one of the larger bets a US lab has placed on a Chinese-founded agent startup and signals that any deal touching Chinese talent or technology now carries regulatory risk that can undo it after the fact. Founders raising from or selling to US acquirers should price in that a foreign government, not just antitrust regulators, can kill the transaction.

title: Sarvam becomes India’s newest AI unicorn with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech
blurb: Sarvam joined India's unicorn club with a $234M round led by HCLTech, which is putting in $150M of the total. The deal ties the Bengaluru startup to one of India's largest IT services firms, giving it an enterprise distribution channel and a strategic backer rather than a purely financial one. India's AI buildout is increasingly running through its IT incumbents, which are funding homegrown model labs to defend their services business against the same automation they sell.

title: All the news about Anthropic’s new AI fight with the White House
blurb: Anthropic is now fighting on two government fronts at once, with a Pentagon standoff already underway and a June 12th White House order demanding it block foreign access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5, the frontier models it shipped just three days earlier. The clash signals that export-style controls are moving from chips to model weights themselves, with Washington treating the most capable systems as strategic assets. For any founder building on frontier APIs, the lesson is that your model supplier's access can now be reshaped overnight by national security policy you don't control.

title: Salesforce to Acquire Fin (formerly Intercom) for $3.6B
blurb: Salesforce signed a definitive agreement to acquire Fin, the AI customer-service company formerly known as Intercom, for $3.6B. The deal folds an AI support agent that resolves tickets autonomously straight into Salesforce's Service Cloud, where the incumbent has been losing ground to startups that rebuilt support around AI from scratch. The pattern is familiar: rather than out-ship the AI-native challengers, the platform giants are buying them, and the price of staying independent in customer service just got a public benchmark.

title: Source: Anthropic was given 90 minutes to comply and was not provided with detailed concerns before the export control order was issued (Financial Times)
blurb: Anthropic was reportedly given 90 minutes to comply with a US export control order and was not shown detailed concerns before it was issued, according to the Financial Times. The compressed timeline signals that frontier labs now operate under regulatory pressure that can shift faster than any compliance process can absorb. For founders building on these labs, the lesson is that your core supplier sits inside a policy environment where rules can change in the span of a single afternoon, with little warning and even less room to push back.

title: Salesforce acquires AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6B
blurb: Salesforce acquired Fin, an AI customer service platform, for $3.6B, folding its team and technology into Agentforce. The deal buys Salesforce a proven autonomous support agent rather than building one from scratch, and it deepens the bet that customer service is the first enterprise workflow AI agents fully own. Expect the frontier of competition in CRM to keep shifting from software seats to agents that resolve tickets, with pricing tied to outcomes instead of headcount.

title: As AI agents become employees, NewCore emerges with $66M to give them identities
blurb: NewCore launched with $66M to issue and govern identities for AI agents, betting that the next enterprise security problem is managing software workers rather than human ones. As agents start taking actions inside systems the way employees do, they need credentials, permissions, and audit trails that the existing identity stack was never built to handle. For founders, it marks the start of a new infrastructure layer forming around agentic AI, where access control and accountability become the choke point that determines how far companies are willing to deploy agents.

title: As AI companies race to go public, who else is along for the ride?
blurb:
I'll write from the provided context rather than wait on the fetch.

The IPO window is reopening on the back of a handful of AI and space names, and the companies that benefit most aren't the ones going public. When a marquee listing like SpaceX prices well, it lifts comparables, frees up late-stage capital, and gives crossover investors a reason to fund the next round, so adjacent startups ride the sentiment without ever filing. The play for founders is to recognize that a hot IPO is a market signal, not just one company's exit, and to raise while the window is open rather than after it closes again.

title: Canadian payments company Nuvei agrees to acquire NY-based cross-border payments company Payoneer for ~$2.75B in cash; the deal is expected to close in mid-2027 (Reuters)
blurb: Nuvei agreed to acquire Payoneer for about $2.75B in cash, pulling the cross-border payments platform private under the Canadian processor's umbrella. The deal isn't expected to close until mid-2027, an unusually long runway that signals heavy regulatory review across the many jurisdictions Payoneer operates in. For founders running global businesses, it's another sign that the payments stack is consolidating fast, with the infrastructure layer you depend on increasingly owned by a handful of acquisitive processors.

title: AI OSS tool repo goes archived over night after raising $7.3M Seed
blurb: TensorZero, the open-source LLMOps platform that routes roughly 1% of global LLM API spend, archived its public GitHub repo overnight shortly after closing a $7.3M seed round. The move pulls a tool used by frontier startups and Fortune 10 companies behind a commercial wall, leaving teams that built on its unified gateway, observability, and optimization layers to fork the last open commit or migrate. The pattern is familiar: venture money lands, and the open-source project that won the users gets reclassified as the funnel, not the product.

title: SpaceX IPO raised a total of $85.7B as underwriters exercised their overallotment of shares, after raising an initial $75B on June 11 (CNBC)
blurb: SpaceX's IPO raised $85.7B after underwriters exercised their overallotment, on top of the $75B priced June 11. That makes it the largest public offering on record and values the most valuable private-company-turned-public well past anything the venture market has seen. The signal for founders is that the exit window at the very top is wide open again, and the capital chasing frontier hardware, launch, and satellite businesses now has a benchmark to point at when underwriting the next decade of deep-tech bets.

title: Amazon CEO's talks with U.S. officials triggered crackdown on Anthropic models
blurb: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy's conversations with U.S. officials reportedly set off a government crackdown on the use of Anthropic's models, according to the Wall Street Journal. The detail that matters is the source of the friction: Amazon is one of Anthropic's largest backers, yet the report shows a strategic investor working channels in ways that can cut against the company it funds. For founders building on a frontier lab, the lesson is that your biggest backer and your biggest competitive threat are increasingly the same party, and their government relationships are now part of your risk surface.

title: Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
blurb: Anthropic took its newest Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models offline over the weekend after the White House demanded it block all foreign nationals, including the company's own overseas employees. The episode handed sovereign-AI advocates fresh ammunition, with the UK framing domestic AI capacity as national security and France's Gabriel Attal calling it the start of "the AI war" and comparing US leverage to a Strait of Hormuz chokepoint. The lesson for founders building on American frontier models is that access is now a geopolitical variable, not a contractual one.

title: Radical Numerics, which is developing AI models that learn directly from biological data, raised a $50M seed led by Emergence Capital (Natalie Breymeyer/Axios)
blurb: Radical Numerics raised a $50M seed led by Emergence Capital to build AI models that learn directly from biological data. The bet is that biology, not text scraped from the web, is the dataset where the next set of breakthroughs hides, and that models trained natively on it will read signals general-purpose systems miss. A seed of that size signals investors are willing to fund foundation-model economics in vertical scientific domains, not just the general frontier.

title: NewCore, which helps companies manage both human and AI agent identities in a single system, emerges from stealth with a $66M seed led by Cyberstarts (Jagmeet Singh/TechCrunch)
blurb: NewCore emerged from stealth with a $66M seed led by Cyberstarts to manage human and AI agent identities in one system. The pitch lands because most identity stacks were built for people, and they buckle once autonomous agents start authenticating, acting, and accessing data at machine speed and volume. A seed round that large signals investors expect agent identity to become its own security category, the way IAM did for the cloud era.

title: American Express agrees to acquire restaurant booking platform TheFork from Tripadvisor in an all-cash deal worth $700M; TRIP jumps 6%+ (Reuters)
blurb: American Express agreed to acquire TheFork, the European restaurant-booking platform, from Tripadvisor in an all-cash deal worth $700M. The purchase extends Amex's dining business beyond reservations and reviews into the booking layer where its cardholders already spend, deepening a strategy of owning the experiences its premium customers pay for. Tripadvisor shares jumped more than 6% on the news, a sign investors welcomed the divestiture of a unit that fit awkwardly inside a reviews-and-travel company.

title: European tech has discovered some swagger
blurb: European tech has spent years apologizing for not being Silicon Valley, building quietly and pricing modestly while founders defaulted to the assumption that the real action happened elsewhere. That posture is fading, as a generation of operators who have built and sold at scale now start companies expecting to win globally rather than get acquired early. The shift that matters is not capital or talent, which Europe already had, but the willingness to make ambitious bets out loud and defend them.

title: Arcade, which helps companies manage which actions AI agents are authorized to take, raised a $60M Series A led by SYN Ventures, following a $12M seed in 2025 (Steven Rosenbush/Wall Street Journal)
blurb: Arcade raised a $60M Series A led by SYN Ventures, a year after closing a $12M seed, to control which actions AI agents are authorized to take. The round size signals where the market is heading: as companies move agents from demos into production, the bottleneck shifts from model capability to permissioning and access control. The smart read for founders is that agent infrastructure is splitting into a real stack, and authorization is becoming its own category rather than a feature buried inside the orchestration layer.

title: Orbio raises $21 million to automate hiring and onboarding for frontline workers
blurb: Orbio raised a $21M Series A led by Dawn Capital to automate hiring and management of frontline workers with AI agents. The pitch is a closed loop: agents named Maria, Daniel, and Claire interview candidates, monitor output, and run daily check-ins, with each agent's data feeding the others so exit interviews recalibrate hiring criteria. Early customers include YUM! Brands and The Stepping Stones Group, where Orbio now runs the full U.S. operation and pushes 20% more candidates through to hire. The real competitor isn't Paradox or WorkJam, it's the spreadsheets and phone calls still managing 2.7 billion frontline workers.

title: A US judge dismisses xAI's lawsuit alleging OpenAI stole trade secrets, saying xAI failed to show OpenAI induced a former xAI engineer to divulge trade secrets (Jonathan Stempel/Reuters)
blurb: A US judge dismissed xAI's lawsuit claiming OpenAI stole trade secrets through a former xAI engineer, ruling that xAI failed to show OpenAI induced the engineer to share confidential information. The case is one of several legal fronts between Elon Musk and OpenAI, and this one collapsed on the evidentiary bar rather than the merits of whether secrets moved. For founders, it is a reminder that poaching claims rarely stick without proof of active inducement, which is hard to document when an engineer simply walks out the door.

title: Zhipu's shares closed up 33% on Monday after JPMorgan raised the stock's price target and picked it as a winner against MiniMax; MiniMax closed up 7% (Bloomberg)
blurb: Zhipu's shares closed up 33% on Monday after JPMorgan lifted its price target and named the Chinese AI lab the likely winner over rival MiniMax, which still rose 7%. The split call signals that Wall Street is starting to handicap China's foundation-model race rather than treat the whole cohort as one bet. For founders building on or against Chinese models, the capital is now consolidating behind specific labs, and the gap between perceived winners and the rest will only widen from here.

title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
blurb: Attackers tampered with trusted JavaScript files distributed by the popular WordPress plugins PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse, turning them into a vector for site compromise. The injected code triggers only when a logged-in administrator loads the file, silently creating an attacker-controlled admin account and installing a hidden plugin that opens a persistent backdoor while leaving ordinary visitors untouched.

title: Show HN: Kage – Shadow any website to a single binary for offline viewing
blurb: Kage crawls any website and packages it into a single self-contained binary that serves the full site offline with no external dependencies. The Go tool captures HTML, CSS, JS, and assets into one executable you can run anywhere, useful for archiving docs, demoing sites without a network, or shipping a frozen snapshot.

title: The adder at the heart of Intel's 8087 floating-point chip
blurb: This reverse-engineering deep dive dissects the 69-bit adder at the core of Intel's 1980 8087 floating-point coprocessor, the chip that accelerated math up to 100x and computed transcendental functions like tangent, exponentiation, and logarithms. The post explains how the adder sidesteps slow carry propagation by splitting addition into 4-bit blocks, cutting the carry path by a factor of four, with each sum bit produced by XORing the fraction bus, B register, and incoming carry.

title: RTX 5080 and RTX 3090 Setup: 80 Tok/s on Qwen 3.6 27B Q8
blurb: The author combined an RTX 5080 (16GB) and a refurbished RTX 3090 (24GB) on an Asus Prime X570-Pro to hit 80+ tok/s running Qwen 3.6 27B at Q8. The post details the required BIOS changes, including disabling CSM, enabling Above 4G Decoding and ReSize BAR, and splitting the PCIe x16 slot into 2x8, plus the NVIDIA driver caveats that prevent P2P on mismatched GPU generations.

title: Accelerating researchers and developers building multilingual AI with a new open dataset
blurb: GitHub published a new repository-level dataset under CC0-1.0 that maps multilingual developer content across READMEs, issues, and pull requests. The release gives researchers and developers a public-domain corpus for training and evaluating multilingual AI models on real-world software discussions rather than English-only sources.

title: 21 years and counting of 'eight fallacies of distributed computing' (2025)
blurb: The post revisits Peter Deutsch's eight fallacies of distributed computing and argues they remain accurate 21 years on, with engineers still building as if the network is reliable, latency is zero, and bandwidth is infinite. The author maps each fallacy to modern failure modes in microservices and cloud systems, noting that newer abstractions hide the network without removing its costs.

title: Free SQL→ER diagram tool, runs in the browser, nothing uploaded
blurb: This browser-based tool converts SQL schema definitions into entity-relationship diagrams entirely client-side, with no server upload or account required. The parsing and rendering run locally, so database schemas never leave the machine, and the tool is free to use for visualizing table structures and foreign-key relationships.

title: AI OSS tool repo goes archived over night after raising $7.3M Seed
blurb: TensorZero, an open-source LLMOps platform that routes roughly 1% of global LLM API spend through its Rust-based gateway, archived its public GitHub repo shortly after closing a $7.3M seed round. The move signals a likely shift away from open source toward a commercial product, leaving teams that adopted the gateway, observability, and evaluation stack to weigh forking the last open release or migrating off.

title: AI demands more engineering discipline. Not less (xpost)
blurb: Charity Majors argues that AI coding tools raise the bar for engineering discipline rather than lowering it, comparing the moment to the shift from hand-tended server pets to immutable infrastructure. She contends that faster code generation makes practices like testing, observability, and tight feedback loops more essential, since the cost of shipping unreviewed or poorly understood changes scales with how quickly you can produce them.

title: Caddy compatibility for zeroserve: 3x throughput and 70% lower latency
blurb: zeroserve added a compatibility layer for Caddy configs, letting users run existing Caddyfiles on its server while reporting 3x higher throughput and 70% lower latency in the author's benchmarks. The post details the drop-in migration path and where behavior diverges from Caddy, with the main trade-off being incomplete coverage of Caddy's plugin ecosystem.

title: Don't trust large context windows
blurb: The author argues that large context windows degrade model performance well before hitting their advertised token limits, with recall and reasoning falling off as the prompt fills. The practical advice is to treat context as a scarce budget, aggressively prune irrelevant tokens, and split work across focused calls rather than dumping everything into one oversized window.

title: The experience of rendering Arabic typography and its technical debt
blurb: The author walks through why rendering Arabic typography correctly is harder than it looks, using a "ragged left" justification bug as the entry point into deeper failures: unjoined letters from a PDF library predating its runtime's shaping engine, and a search index returning nothing because a 2017 import stored names in deprecated 1991 Unicode codepoints. The interactive post argues these surface tickets all sit on the same iceberg of text-shaping and encoding debt.

title: Your ePub Is fine
blurb: **The author traces why a spec-compliant ePub gets rejected by Kobo e-readers, pinning the failure on Adobe's aging Reader Mobile SDK rather than the file itself.** The post walks through validating the ePub against the standard, reproducing Kobo's parse error, and showing how the embedded Adobe XML parser chokes on markup that EPUBCheck and other readers accept without complaint.

title: Cybersecurity startup Chainguard, Cisco, Cloudflare, JPMorgan Chase, and others launch Athena, a coalition to secure open-source software using AI (Rachel Metz/Bloomberg)
blurb: Chainguard, Cisco, Cloudflare, and JPMorgan Chase launched Athena, a coalition using AI to secure open-source software.

title: ReactOS (FOSS "Windows") achieves 3D-accelerated Half-Life on real hardware
blurb: ReactOS, the open-source Windows-compatible OS in development for 28 years, ran Half-Life with 3D acceleration on real hardware for the first time.

title: Show HN: I wrote a C++ ray tracer from scratch without AI
blurb: A developer built a C++ ray tracer from scratch by hand, no AI assistance, with the full source on GitHub.

title: LiteLLM Vulnerability Chain Enables Full AI Gateway Takeover from Default Account
blurb: Three chained vulnerabilities in LiteLLM, a widely deployed open-source AI gateway, let a default low-privilege user bypass authorization, escalate to admin, and run arbitrary code for full server takeover, exposing every provider key, decryption secret, and prompt passing through the proxy. Obsidian Security disclosed the CVSS 9.9 chain, and maintainer BerriAI shipped complete fixes in v1.83.14-stable, so anyone running the proxy should upgrade immediately.

title: The Thread Battle: Go Concurrency vs. Node.js Event Loop from First Principles
blurb: This post compares Go's M:N goroutine scheduler against Node.js's single-threaded event loop from the runtime level, tracing Go's design back to the problems Google faced in 2007: heavy OS threads, slow C++ builds, and onboarding complexity. It explains the mechanical reality behind the textbook taglines, covering how each runtime schedules execution across CPU cores and why goroutines stay cheap enough to spin up by the thousands.

title: Yserver: A modern X11 server written in Rust
blurb: Yserver is a modern X11 server written from scratch in Rust that runs full MATE, XFCE, and Cinnamon desktops on standalone DRM/KMS, with support for window managers like FVWM3, e16, and wmaker plus extensions including GLX, RANDR, Composite, and Present. The project deliberately drops legacy baggage such as multiple screens, non-TrueColor visuals, and the DDX driver ABI, and works on AMD, Intel, Asahi, and Qualcomm hardware but never on the Nvidia proprietary driver.

title: Show HN: Paca – Lightweight Jira alternative for human-AI collaboration
blurb: Paca is a self-hosted, open-source project management platform that puts AI agents on the Scrumban board as full teammates rather than bolt-on chatbots, joining sprint planning and picking up backlog tasks. Its core stays small, with workflows, statuses, and board layouts driven by config files and a WASM plugin system that sandboxes extensions behind a capability-based permission model.

title: Introducing Omnigent: A Meta-Harness to Combine, Control and Share Your Agents
blurb: Databricks open sourced Omnigent under Apache 2.0, a meta-harness that sits above existing coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Pi to make them interoperable, composable, and governable from one common interface. The layer targets the problems a single harness can't solve, adding ways to chain multiple agents, apply control policies, and collaborate live with teammates across different models and sessions.

title: How difficult could it be to design a chatbot?
blurb: A study testing culture-tailored chatbot conversation styles across Germany, South Africa, the USA, and India found that users high in horizontal individualism responded better to collectivist framing in a blood-donation context, the opposite of what designers would predict. The takeaway is that prosocial behaviors create a contribution conflict, where personal-benefit framing works against the altruistic motivation driving the action, so conversation design has to match the behavior's intent rather than the user's cultural profile.

title: Every Frame Perfect
blurb: Tonsky argues that UI quality is judged frame by frame: if you screenshot an app at any moment, every element on screen should be explainable, because users read polish as a proxy for engineering care. The piece dissects desynchronized animations in Safari and Photos, where elements that snap and elements that ease apart break the sense that a UI is a precise instrument rather than an animated toy.

title: Cisco fixes SD-WAN vManage flaw exploited in zero-day attacks
blurb: Cisco patched a privilege-escalation flaw in Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (vManage), tracked as CVE-2026-20262, that was exploited as a zero-day to escalate to root. Organizations running affected vManage instances should apply the fixed release immediately and audit appliances for signs of root-level compromise predating the patch.

title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
blurb: Researchers at Varonis Threat Labs chained three bugs into a one-click exfiltration path, dubbed SearchLeak, that let an attacker pull emails, calendar entries, and indexed files from Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search. Because the malicious link resolved to a legitimate microsoft.com domain, conventional anti-phishing and URL filtering missed it, and the attack could also harvest MFA codes before Microsoft remediated the flaw.

title: Public and Private Medical Community Targeted by China-Nexus Threat Actor Pursuing Artificial Intelligence, Cyber, Medical, and National Defense Research
blurb: Google Threat Intelligence Group attributed a campaign to UNC6508, a PRC-nexus actor that spent over a year inside North American academic, medical, and military research institutions, compromising external web apps, deploying bespoke malware, and abusing enterprise admin tools for covert exfiltration. Collection targets spanned AI, uncrewed vehicle systems, offensive cyber programs, Indo-Pacific command operations, and medical research; GTIG disrupted the infrastructure and notified affected organizations with Mandiant.

title: Popular WordPress Plugin Scripts Tampered to Plant Hidden Backdoors on Sites
blurb: Attackers tampered with trusted JavaScript files served by the PushEngage, OptinMonster, and TrustPulse WordPress plugins, turning them into a supply-chain backdoor that activates only when a logged-in administrator loads the script. The injected code silently creates an attacker-controlled admin account and installs a hidden plugin for persistent access, so affected sites should audit admin users, remove rogue plugins, and rotate credentials.

title: FBI disrupts massive AI-powered phishing service using a million URLs
blurb: The FBI, working with Google and Black Lotus Labs, dismantled Outsider Enterprise, a Chinese phishing-as-a-service operation that used AI to generate over a million URLs across thousands of sites to harvest credit card data and credentials. Defenders should pull the published IOCs into blocklists and monitor for credential-stuffing and card-fraud activity tied to data already exfiltrated before the takedown.

title: Arch Linux locks down AUR signups amid wave of malicious commits
blurb: Arch Linux froze new Arch User Repository (AUR) account signups after attackers flooded the community repo with poisoned package updates pushed through freshly created accounts. AUR packages are unvetted build scripts that execute on user systems, so admins who installed or updated AUR packages during the campaign should audit recently pulled PKGBUILDs and rebuild from known-good sources.

title: Council of Europe hacked in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft heist
blurb: The ShinyHunters extortion group breached the Council of Europe by exploiting Oracle PeopleSoft, exfiltrating data in a campaign that also claimed the University of Nottingham and roughly 100 other unnamed organizations. Defenders running internet-facing PeopleSoft should treat the deployments as targeted, apply Oracle's latest patches, and audit access logs for unauthorized data exports tied to the group's activity.

title: U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
blurb: The U.S. government ordered Anthropic to suspend access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all foreign nationals on national security grounds, prompting the company to disable both models entirely while it disputes what it calls a "misunderstanding." The order followed a reported Fable 5 jailbreak, though Anthropic says the technique surfaced only minor, already-known vulnerabilities that other public models can find without a bypass.

title: Arch Linux Now Believes Malware Incident Under Control: More Than 1,500 Packages
blurb: The Arch Linux AUR user-contributed repository was hit by a malware campaign that compromised at least 1,579 packages, escalating from an initial estimate of roughly 400 over the course of a single day. Maintainers have deleted all known malicious commits, but the published list is described as incomplete, so users who recently installed AUR packages should audit their systems and rebuild from clean sources.

title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
blurb: Splunk released updates for CVE-2026-20253 (CVSS 9.8), a critical flaw in Splunk Enterprise versions below 10.2.4 and 10.0.7 where an unauthenticated PostgreSQL sidecar endpoint allows arbitrary file creation and truncation. watchTowr Labs demonstrated a pre-auth RCE chain abusing the /backup and /restore endpoints and a planted .pgpass file, so on-prem deployments should patch immediately; Splunk Cloud is unaffected.

title: Chinese hackers hijack auth flow, spy on isolated network for a decade
blurb: Chinese cyberespionage group Velvet Ant breached the air-gapped critical infrastructure network of a large organization and ran undetected for 10 years in a campaign Sygnia dubbed "Operation Highland," compromising the authentication stack to maintain full visibility into administrative activity. The group pivoted from internet-facing servers into the isolated network by chaining a modified GS-Netcat reverse shell, a SOCKS5 proxy masquerading as 'smbd,' and Nginx-to-FastCGI request forwarding to reach systems with no direct external path.

title: Chinese hackers breach REDCap servers, steal medical research
